source_docs.title: Microbial single-cell RNA sequencing by split-pool barcoding

oligos.name: Round 1 RT primer (poly-dT, barcode 1)
oligos.role: Barcoded reverse-transcription primer (round 1 cell barcode) — polyadenylated-mRNA capture

components.name: 5' phosphate + ligation linker
components.role: 5'-phosphorylated overhang; ligation handle for round-2 barcode

components.name: poly-dT + VN anchor
components.role: anneals to in-cell polyadenylated mRNA 3' tail

oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated. One of 96 round-1 primers; wells A1-D12 (48) are dt(15)VN, wells E1-H12 (48) are random hexamer. The dt and hexamer primer for corresponding wells are pooled for RT.

oligos.name: Round 1 RT primer (random hexamer, barcode 1)
oligos.role: Barcoded reverse-transcription primer (round 1 cell barcode) — random-primed capture

components.name: 5' phosphate + ligation linker
components.role: 5'-phosphorylated overhang; ligation handle for round-2 barcode

components.name: round 1 barcode (8 nt)
components.role: well-specific cell barcode 1 (shares barcode set with the paired poly-dT well)

components.name: random hexamer
components.role: primes anywhere on RNA (captures rRNA/non-polyadenylated as well as mRNA)

oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated. 48 random-hexamer primers (wells E1-H12) pooled 1:1 with the matching poly-dT primer for reverse transcription.

oligos.name: Round 2 ligation barcode (barcode 2)
oligos.role: Second combinatorial cell barcode, appended by in-cell ligation

components.name: 3' constant
components.role: hybridizes to round-2 linker/blocker to ligate onto round-1 ACTGTGG end

oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated. 96 barcodes. Ligated to the round-1 primer 5' end using the round-2 barcode linker (BC_0335) and round-2 blocking strand (BC_0340).

oligos.name: Round 3 ligation barcode + UMI (barcode 3)
oligos.role: Third combinatorial cell barcode with UMI and biotin, appended by in-cell ligation

components.name: 5' biotin + Read 2 handle
components.role: 5'-biotin (/5Biosg/); TruSeq Read 2 priming region for library capture on streptavidin and Read 2 sequencing

components.name: 3' constant / ligation handle
components.role: hybridizes to round-3 linker/blocker to ligate onto round-2 CATCGGCGTACGACT end

oligos.notes: 5'-biotinylated (/5Biosg/). 96 barcodes. UMI = 10 random nt. Ligated to round-2 barcode using round-3 barcode linker (BC_0284) and round-3 blocking strand (BC_0066).

oligos.name: BC_0335 Round 2 barcode linker
oligos.role: Splint/linker bridging round-1 and round-2 barcodes for ligation
oligos.notes: Anneals across the round-1 ACTGTGG end and the round-2 barcode 3' constant to template the ligation.

oligos.name: BC_0340 Round 2 blocking strand
oligos.notes: Blocks unligated ends during the round-2 ligation.

oligos.name: BC_0284 Round 3 barcode linker
oligos.role: Splint/linker bridging round-2 and round-3 barcodes for ligation
oligos.notes: Anneals across the round-2 CATCGGCGTACGACT end and the round-3 barcode 3' constant to template the ligation.

oligos.name: BC_0066 Round 3 blocking strand
oligos.notes: Blocks unligated ends during the round-3 ligation.

oligos.name: BC_0127 Template switch oligo (TSO)
oligos.role: Template-switching primer adding the 5' cDNA PCR handle
oligos.notes: HPLC-purified (Exiqon). Terminal ...GG G = two ribonucleic guanines (rGrG) followed by a locked-nucleic-acid guanine (+G); folded here to GGG. The rG/rG/LNA-G 3' end base-pairs with the untemplated Cs added by Maxima H Minus RT for template switching.

oligos.name: Adaptor duplex (BC_0243 / BC_0244) — TruSeq Read 1 adapter
oligos.role: Double-stranded sequencing adapter ligated to fragmented cDNA (adds Read 1 handle)

components.name: BC_0243 adaptor duplex top strand
components.role: Top strand; TruSeq Read 1 sequence. 3'-terminal T is phosphorothioated (...GATC*T)

components.name: BC_0244 adaptor duplex bottom strand
components.role: Bottom strand (partially complementary; forms Y/forked adapter)

oligos.notes: Pre-annealed at 100 uM in 50 uM NaCl and ligated to fragmented cDNA with WGS Ligase.

oligos.name: BC_0027 PCR primer (P5 / sublibrary amplification)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer — adds P5 and TruSeq Read 1 (transcript/cDNA side)
oligos.notes: P5 flow-cell sequence (AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCT) + TruSeq Read 1 primer (ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT). Primes on the ligated adaptor duplex.

oligos.name: BC_0076-BC_0083 PCR primer (P7 / sublibrary index i7)
oligos.role: Library PCR primer — adds P7 and the 6 nt sublibrary index (barcode 4, i7)

components.name: sublibrary index i7 (6 nt)
components.role: fourth barcode; read in the 6 nt index read

components.name: TruSeq Read 2 region
components.role: primes on the round-3 Read 2 handle (CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT)

oligos.notes: Used with BC_0027. One of BC_0076-BC_0083 per sublibrary; the 6 nt i7 index is the fourth barcode read in the index read.

final_library.source_label: microSPLiT sequencing-ready library (SPLiT-seq architecture: P5–TruSeq R1–cDNA–BC1–BC2–BC3–UMI–TruSeq R2–i7–P7)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT [CDNA] AAAAAAAAAAAAAAA [CELL_BARCODE:8]{BC1} CCACAGTCTCAAGCACGTGGAT [CELL_BARCODE:8]{BC2} AGTCGTACGCCGATGCGAAACATCGGCCAC [CELL_BARCODE:8]{BC3} [UMI:10] AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC [SAMPLE_INDEX:6]{i7} ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'

strands.direction: first-strand cDNA orientation (5'->3', barcode block on cDNA 5' end)

annotation_lines: TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT — TruSeq Read 1 primer site (Read 1 reads the transcript next)
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAA — poly-A (reverse complement of the RT poly-dT capture; absent for hexamer-primed molecules)
CCACAGTCTCAAGCACGTGGAT — round-1/round-2 linker constant (rc of ACTGTGG + ATCCACGTGCTTGAG)
AGTCGTACGCCGATGCGAAACATCGGCCAC — round-2/round-3 linker constant (rc of CATCGGCGTACGACT + GTGGCCGATGTTTCG)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC — TruSeq Read 2 primer site (rc of GTGACTGGAGTTCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT); Read 2 reads UMI + BC3 + BC2 + BC1
ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG — P7 flow-cell sequence (rc of CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGAT)

evidence.locator: microSPLiT sequencing-ready library (SPLiT-seq architecture: P5–TruSeq R1–cDNA–BC1–BC2–BC3–UMI–TruSeq R2–i7–P7)

library_generation.title: Fixation, permeabilization & in-cell polyadenylation
library_generation.summary: Fixed bacteria are permeabilized with Tween-20/lysozyme and their mRNA is polyadenylated in-cell with E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase.
library_generation.note: Gram-positive and gram-negative cells are formaldehyde-fixed, permeabilized with mild detergent (Tween-20) plus lysozyme, and treated with PAP to add 3' poly-A tails, enriching mRNA (~2.5-fold) for downstream poly-dT capture. Bacterial mRNA is otherwise not polyadenylated.

library_generation.title: Round 1 — in-cell reverse transcription (barcode 1)
library_generation.summary: In-cell RT with well-specific barcoded poly-dT and random-hexamer primers writes barcode 1 into first-strand cDNA.
library_generation.note: Cells are split into a 96-well plate; each well's RT primer carries a unique 8 nt barcode plus a 5'-phosphate/ACTGTGG ligation handle. Poly-dT and random-hexamer primers of the same well share a barcode and are pooled. Maxima H Minus RT adds untemplated Cs at the cDNA 3' end.
library_generation.product:
5'- [mRNA] AAAAA...A --------3'
            ||||||
        3'- VN(T)15 [BC1:8] GGTGTCA -Phos-5'   (round-1 primer)

first-strand cDNA (5'->3'):
5'-Phos- ACTGTGG [BC1:8] TTTTTTTTTTTTTTT [cDNA] CCC -3'

library_generation.title: Round 2 — in-cell ligation (barcode 2)
library_generation.summary: Cells are pooled, re-split, and an 8 nt barcode-2 oligo is ligated onto the cDNA 5' end via a splint linker.
library_generation.note: The round-2 barcode linker (BC_0335) and blocking strand (BC_0340) bridge the round-1 ACTGTGG end and the round-2 barcode oligo (5'-P-CATCGGCGTACGACT-[BC2]-ATCCACGTGCTTGAG) for ligation.
library_generation.product:
5'-Phos- CATCGGCGTACGACT [BC2:8] ATCCACGTGCTTGAG | ACTGTGG [BC1:8] T15 [cDNA] -3'
                                                 ^ligation junction (round-2 linker splint)

library_generation.title: Round 3 — in-cell ligation (barcode 3 + UMI + biotin)
library_generation.summary: A third ligation appends barcode 3, a 10 nt UMI, the Read 2 handle and a 5' biotin.
library_generation.note: Round-3 oligo: 5'-Biotin-CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT-[UMI:10]-[BC3:8]-GTGGCCGATGTTTCG. The round-3 barcode linker (BC_0284) and blocker (BC_0066) template ligation onto the round-2 CATCGGCGTACGACT end. Each cell now has a unique BC1+BC2+BC3 combination.

library_generation.title: Lysis, streptavidin capture & template switching
library_generation.summary: Cells are lysed, biotinylated cDNA is bound to streptavidin beads, and template switching adds the 5' cDNA PCR handle.
library_generation.note: The template-switch oligo BC_0127 (AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTGAATrGrG+G) anneals to the untemplated Cs at the cDNA 3' end; Maxima H Minus RT extends to append the SMART handle used for on-bead PCR amplification of full-length barcoded cDNA.
library_generation.product:
5'-Bio- CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT [UMI:10][BC3][linker][BC2][linker][BC1] T15 [cDNA] CCCATTCACTCTGCGTTGATACCACTGCTT -3'
                                                                              (template-switch handle at cDNA 3' end)

library_generation.title: Fragmentation & Read 1 adapter ligation
library_generation.summary: Amplified cDNA is enzymatically fragmented and a TruSeq Read 1 adaptor duplex (BC_0243/BC_0244) is ligated to the new ends.
library_generation.note: WGS fragmentation mix cuts the cDNA; double-sided SPRI size selection is followed by WGS-Ligase ligation of the pre-annealed forked adaptor, placing the TruSeq Read 1 sequence adjacent to the transcript so Read 1 will read cDNA.
library_generation.product:
5'- ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT [cDNA fragment] ...[BC1][BC2][BC3][UMI] CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT -Bio-3'
     (ligated TruSeq Read 1 adapter)

library_generation.title: Indexing PCR (P5 / P7 + sublibrary index)
library_generation.summary: qPCR with BC_0027 (P5) and one of BC_0076-BC_0083 adds P5, the 6 nt sublibrary index (barcode 4) and P7 to yield the final library.
library_generation.note: BC_0027 primes on the ligated Read 1 adapter (adds P5); BC_0076-BC_0083 prime on the round-3 Read 2 handle (add the i7 sublibrary index + P7). Double-sided SPRI (0.5x-0.7x) gives the sequencing-ready library.
library_generation.product: 5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC TCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT [CDNA] A15 [BC1] linker [BC2] linker [BC3] [UMI] AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC [i7:6] ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'

library_sequencing.note: Sequences into the cDNA insert (the captured transcript). Primer anneals to the bottom strand at the TruSeq Read 1 handle and extends toward the poly-A / BC1 block.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 sequencing primer
            5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------->
top:    5'- ...ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[cDNA]AAAAAAAAAAAAAAA[BC1]... -3'
bottom: 3'- ...TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA[cDNA]TTTTTTTTTTTTTTT[BC1]... -5'
               (primer = top-strand sequence; it anneals to the bottom strand,
                which is the template, and reads 74 nt of cDNA)

library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6 nt sublibrary index (the 4th barcode, added during indexing PCR). The index primer anneals to the bottom strand at the Read 2 handle, its 3' end abutting the i7, and reads toward P7.
library_sequencing.diagram:
i7 index-read primer
                     5'-GATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC------->
top:    5'- ...[UMI:10]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[i7:6]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
bottom: 3'- ...[UMI']  TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG[i7'] TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
               (primer = top-strand handle sequence; anneals to the bottom strand,
                which is the template, and reads the 6 nt i7 index toward P7)

library_sequencing.read: Read 2 — UMI + cell barcodes (BC3, BC2, BC1)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 10 nt UMI, then BC3, BC2, BC1 (8 nt each) through the two ligation-linker constants. Primer anneals to the top-strand Read 2 handle and extends leftward (toward the cDNA end).
library_sequencing.diagram:
top:    5'- [BC1]CCACAGTCTCAAGCACGTGGAT[BC2]AGTCGTACGCCGATGCGAAACATCGGCCAC[BC3][UMI:10]AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTGAACTCCAGTCAC[i7]... -3'
bottom: 3'- [BC1]GGTGTCAGAGTTCGTGCACCTA[BC2]TCAGCATGCGGCTACGCTTTGTAGCCGGTG[BC3][UMI']  TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG[i7]... -5'
            <-------------------------------------------------------------- Read 2 (86 nt)
                                                                                    3'-TCTAGCCTTCTCGTGTGCAGACTTGAGGTCAGTG-5'
                                                        (primer 5'-GTGACTGG...CCGATCT-3' anneals to
                                                         the top strand = template; reads UMI+BC3+BC2+BC1)

title: microSPLiT
description: microSPLiT (Microbial Split-Pool Ligation Transcriptomics) is a high-throughput, droplet-free bacterial single-cell RNA-seq method that extends SPLiT-seq to gram-positive and gram-negative bacteria. Fixed, Tween-20/lysozyme-permeabilized cells are polyadenylated in-cell with E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase, then labeled by combinatorial barcoding: in-cell reverse transcription with barcoded poly-dT and random-hexamer primers (barcode 1), followed by two in-cell ligation rounds (barcodes 2 and 3, the last also carrying a UMI and biotin), and a fourth sublibrary index added during library preparation. Read 1 captures the transcript sequence, Read 2 captures the UMI plus the three cell barcodes, and the index read captures the sublibrary barcode, so every cell acquires a unique barcode combination.

reference.label: Microbial single-cell RNA sequencing by split-pool barcoding

original_publication.title: Microbial single-cell RNA sequencing by split-pool barcoding

throughput.summary: High-throughput, droplet-free; >25,000 B. subtilis cells profiled across a growth curve; species-mixing experiment on 2,677 cells with 99.2% unambiguous species assignment.
throughput.cells: 25,214 B. subtilis cells (combined growth-curve dataset); ~2,677 cells in the E. coli/B. subtilis species-mixing experiment; scalable toward millions of cells
throughput.rna: median ~237 unique mRNA transcripts/cell (E. coli) and ~376/cell (B. subtilis), ~5-10% of estimated total mRNA; 3,717 genes detected for E. coli, 3,476 for B. subtilis

publication.statistical_model: UMI-based digital counting; Scanpy normalization/scaling, top-16-PC neighborhood graph, Louvain graph clustering, and t-SNE / UMAP embedding; cluster marker genes by Wilcoxon rank-sum test with Benjamini-Hochberg correction.

other.label: Method name
other.value: microSPLiT = Microbial Split-Pool Ligation Transcriptomics; extends SPLiT-seq (Rosenberg et al. 2018) to bacteria

other.label: mRNA enrichment
other.value: In-cell polyadenylation with E. coli Poly(A) Polymerase I (PAP) — best of the tested enrichment strategies

other.label: Permeabilization
other.value: Mild detergent Tween-20 + lysozyme; mild sonication after RT to break aggregates

other.label: Sequencing platform
other.value: Illumina MiSeq or NextSeq, 150 nt kits, paired-end; R1 74 nt, R2 86 nt, index 6 nt

other.label: Key enzyme
other.value: Maxima H Minus Reverse Transcriptase (ThermoFisher) with a template-switch oligo (rGrG+G LNA)

other.label: Biological findings
other.value: Detected rare states — competence (K-state, ~4.6% of late-OD cells), PBSX prophage induction (36 cells, 0.142%), and heterogeneous myo-inositol catabolism

other.label: Preprint
other.value: bioRxiv 2019, doi:10.1101/869248 (posted 11 Dec 2019); Science First Release 17 Dec 2020

other.label: Commercialization
other.value: C. Roco, A.B. Rosenberg and G. Seelig co-founded Split Biosciences; a patent on microSPLiT was filed

data_processing.summary: Reads are demultiplexed by the four combinatorial barcodes, aligned to the reference genome (a combined B. subtilis–E. coli genome for the species-mixing experiment), and collapsed by UMI into a per-cell gene count matrix; cells are then normalized/scaled, dimensionally reduced, graph-clustered, and embedded for visualization, with cluster marker genes called statistically.
data_processing.statistical_model: UMI-based digital counting; cluster marker genes by Wilcoxon rank-sum test with Benjamini–Hochberg correction (no parametric count model specified)
